title: Getting Lost in an Unfamiliar City

all.en: On my first day in Paris，I confidently set out alone to explore the famous neighborhoods.
all.zh: 在巴黎的第一天，我充滿自信地獨自出門去探索著名的街區。

all.en: I had a paper map in my bag，but I wanted to rely on my sense of direction.
all.zh: 我包包裡有一張紙本地圖，但我想依靠自己的方向感。

all.en: After taking several wrong turns，I realized I had no idea where I was.
all.zh: 轉錯了好幾次彎之後，我才意識到自己完全不知道身在何處。

all.en: The street signs were in French，and my phone battery had almost run out.
all.zh: 路標都是法文，而且我的手機電量幾乎用完了。

all.en: I tried to stay calm and looked for a familiar landmark in the distance.
all.zh: 我試著保持冷靜，並在遠處尋找熟悉的地標。

all.en: After walking for thirty minutes，I found myself in a quiet residential area.
all.zh: 走了三十分鐘後，我發現自己來到了一個安靜的住宅區。

all.en: An elderly woman noticed my confused face and asked if I needed any help.
all.zh: 一位年長的女士注意到我困惑的表情，問我是否需要幫助。

all.en: Although she did not speak much English，she drew a simple map on a piece of paper.
all.zh: 雖然她不太會說英文，但她在一張紙上畫了一張簡單的地圖。

all.en: She also walked me to the nearest metro station to make sure I was safe.
all.zh: 她還陪我走到最近的地鐵站，確保我安全無虞。

all.en: Her kindness made me feel welcome in a city where I knew no one.
all.zh: 她的善意讓我在這個一個人都不認識的城市感到溫暖。

all.en: Now I always carry a paper map and learn a few useful phrases before traveling.
all.zh: 現在我旅行前都會帶紙本地圖，並學幾句實用的當地話。

all.en: The next morning, I returned to the same neighborhood with a small gift for the kind woman.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我帶著一份小禮物回到同一個街區，要送給那位親切的女士。

all.en: I knocked on her door, but a younger woman answered and told me she had gone shopping.
all.zh: 我敲了她家的門，但一位較年輕的女士來應門，說她去買東西了。

all.en: I left the gift with a thank-you note written carefully in simple French.
all.zh: 我留下了禮物和一張用簡單法文小心寫下的感謝紙條。

all.en: That afternoon, I downloaded a useful offline map application onto my phone.
all.zh: 那天下午，我在手機上下載了一個實用的離線地圖應用程式。

all.en: I also bought a portable charger from a small electronics shop near the hotel.
all.zh: 我也在飯店附近的小電子用品店買了一個行動電源。

all.en: My confidence returned, and I explored several more neighborhoods over the next few days.
all.zh: 我恢復了信心，並在接下來幾天又探索了好幾個街區。

all.en: I discovered hidden bakeries and tiny art galleries that no guidebook ever mentioned.
all.zh: 我發現了一些連旅遊書都沒提到的隱藏麵包店和小型藝廊。

all.en: On my last evening, I went back to thank the woman in person before flying home.
all.zh: 在我搭機回家前的最後一晚，我親自回去當面感謝那位女士。

all.en: She invited me in for tea and showed me old photographs of her younger years in Paris.
all.zh: 她請我進屋喝茶，並給我看她年輕時在巴黎拍的舊照片。

all.en: That random encounter became the most meaningful memory of my entire Paris trip.
all.zh: 那次偶然的相遇成為我整趟巴黎之旅最有意義的回憶。

all.en: A year after my Paris trip, I returned to the same city with my younger brother.
all.zh: 巴黎之旅一年後，我帶著弟弟回到了同一座城市。

all.en: I confidently navigated the metro, showing him every shortcut I had learned before.
all.zh: 我自信地搭乘地鐵，向他展示我以前學會的每一條捷徑。

all.en: We visited the elderly woman together, and she remembered me with a delighted smile.
all.zh: 我們一起去拜訪那位年長的女士，她開心地笑著認出了我。

all.en: She baked us a fresh apple tart and shared more old stories from her younger years.
all.zh: 她為我們烤了一個新鮮的蘋果塔，並分享更多她年輕時的故事。

all.en: My brother was inspired by her kindness and decided to learn French this autumn.
all.zh: 我弟弟被她的善意所感動，決定今年秋天開始學法文。

all.en: We explored the same hidden bakeries I had discovered after my embarrassing first day.
all.zh: 我們走訪了那些我在尷尬的第一天之後發現的隱藏麵包店。

all.en: This time, my phone battery never dropped below fifty percent during the whole trip.
all.zh: 這一次，我手機電量在整趟旅行中從未低於百分之五十。

all.en: I taught my brother to memorize at least one landmark on every street he walked.
all.zh: 我教弟弟在每條走過的街道上至少記住一個地標。

all.en: On our final night, we returned to thank the kind woman with flowers and chocolates.
all.zh: 最後一晚，我們回去送花和巧克力，感謝那位親切的女士。

all.en: That second Paris trip proved that real friendship can grow from a single act of kindness.
all.zh: 第二次的巴黎之旅證明了真摯的友誼可以從一個善舉中萌芽。

all.en: A few years later, I moved to Paris for a six-month language exchange program.
all.zh: 幾年後，我搬到巴黎參加為期六個月的語言交換計畫。

all.en: The elderly woman, now eighty-five, welcomed me back with open arms and warm soup.
all.zh: 那位現在已八十五歲的女士敞開雙臂、用熱湯歡迎我回來。

all.en: I rented a small apartment just two blocks from her quiet residential street.
all.zh: 我在她安靜的住宅街道隔兩條街處租了一間小公寓。

all.en: Every Sunday afternoon, I visited her with fresh pastries from her favorite bakery.
all.zh: 每個星期天下午，我都會帶著她最愛的麵包店的新鮮糕點去拜訪她。

all.en: She taught me how to make her famous apple tart and her secret onion soup recipe.
all.zh: 她教我如何做她著名的蘋果塔和祕傳的洋蔥湯食譜。

all.en: My French improved so much that I started writing short essays about her stories.
all.zh: 我的法文進步神速，開始寫關於她故事的短篇散文。

all.en: A local magazine published one of my essays and praised the depth of our friendship.
all.zh: 當地一家雜誌刊登了我的其中一篇散文，並稱讚我們友誼的深度。

all.en: When she passed away peacefully that winter, her family invited me to her quiet funeral.
all.zh: 那年冬天她安詳地離世，她的家人邀請我參加她寧靜的葬禮。

all.en: They gave me her old handwritten map as the most precious keepsake of our bond.
all.zh: 他們把她那張手繪舊地圖送給我，作為我們情誼最珍貴的紀念。

all.en: I framed that map and hung it where every visitor would ask about its remarkable story.
all.zh: 我把那張地圖裱框，掛在每位訪客都會詢問其動人故事的位置。

all.en: Years after the funeral, I still visited the kind woman's neighborhood every spring.
all.zh: 葬禮過後多年，我每年春天仍會造訪那位親切女士的街區。

all.en: Her daughter and I had become close friends who shared long afternoon walks together.
all.zh: 她的女兒和我成了親密的朋友，常常一起散步度過漫長的午後。

all.en: We organized a small annual gathering to honor her mother and remember her gentle wisdom.
all.zh: 我們籌辦了一場小型的年度聚會，紀念她的母親並回顧她溫柔的智慧。

all.en: My French essays were collected into a small book about kindness between strangers.
all.zh: 我的法文散文被集結成一本小書，講述陌生人之間的善意。

all.en: The local magazine continued publishing my stories every month for several happy years.
all.zh: 那家當地雜誌持續每個月刊登我的故事，幸福地持續了好幾年。

all.en: My brother, now fluent in French, moved to Paris to work for a small architecture firm.
all.zh: 我弟弟法文已能流利溝通，搬到巴黎在一家小型建築事務所工作。

all.en: He visited her grave every Sunday with a single fresh rose, just as I had asked.
all.zh: 他每個星期天都會帶一朵新鮮的玫瑰去她墓前，正如我所託付的。

all.en: Together we created a small scholarship in her name for young French language learners.
all.zh: 我們一起以她的名字設立了一項小型獎學金，獎助年輕的法文學習者。

all.en: Every year, ten students from Asia received help to study in the city she had loved most.
all.zh: 每年，有十位亞洲學生獲得協助，前往她最深愛的城市就學。

all.en: Her handwritten map still hung above my desk, quietly guiding me through every new chapter.
all.zh: 她那張手繪地圖仍掛在我的書桌上方，靜靜地引導我走過每個新篇章。

all.en: Each year, the scholarship recipients wrote heartfelt letters describing their first weeks in her beloved city.
all.zh: 每年，獎學金得主都寫來真摯信件，描述他們在她心愛城市最初幾週的生活。

all.en: I read every letter slowly, feeling her gentle spirit walking the same streets through new students.
all.zh: 我慢慢讀每一封信，感受到她溫柔的靈魂透過新學生再次走在同樣的街道上。

all.en: One scholarship recipient eventually became a translator and helped publish her old French diary in Chinese.
all.zh: 一位獎學金得主後來成為翻譯，協助將她以前的法文日記譯成中文出版。

all.en: The diary, full of small kindnesses, sold thousands of copies across French-language schools in Taiwan.
all.zh: 這本充滿小小善意的日記，在臺灣的法語學校售出數千本。

all.en: Royalties from the book quietly doubled the scholarship fund, allowing twenty new students each year.
all.zh: 書籍版稅靜靜地讓獎學金基金翻倍，讓每年能資助二十位新學生。

all.en: I traveled back to her city for the tenth anniversary of our small but determined scholarship.
all.zh: 我為我們這個雖小但堅定的獎學金十週年特地回到她的城市。

all.en: Her old neighborhood café displayed a corner devoted to her photographs and her handwritten map.
all.zh: 她以前的鄰里咖啡館闢出一角，展示她的照片和那張手繪地圖。

all.en: Former scholarship recipients met me at the café, and we shared one long candlelit dinner together.
all.zh: 以前的獎學金得主在咖啡館與我會合，我們一起共享了一頓漫長的燭光晚餐。

all.en: Walking back to my hotel that night, I noticed I no longer needed her map to find my way.
all.zh: 那晚走回飯店時，我發現我已不再需要她的地圖就能找到路。

all.en: Getting lost long ago had quietly become the truest direction of my entire adult life.
all.zh: 多年前的迷路，已悄悄成為我整個成年人生最真實的方向。

all.en: The following spring, her daughter and I co-curated a small exhibition about Parisian neighborly kindness.
all.zh: 隔年春天，我和她的女兒共同策展了一個關於巴黎鄰里善意的小型展覽。

all.en: Local schoolchildren contributed drawings of strangers helping each other on quiet Paris streets.
all.zh: 當地小學生畫了陌生人在寧靜巴黎街道上互相幫助的圖畫作為展品。

all.en: The mayor's office invited us to display the exhibition in the historic city hall for one whole month.
all.zh: 市長辦公室邀請我們把展覽放在歷史悠久的市政廳展出整整一個月。

all.en: Visitors left handwritten notes describing strangers who had once gently rescued their own lost days.
all.zh: 訪客留下手寫紙條，描述曾經溫柔解救他們迷失日子的陌生人。

all.en: We collected over two thousand notes and bound them into a small archive for future generations.
all.zh: 我們收集了超過兩千張紙條，裝訂成一份小型檔案留給後世。

all.en: My brother designed a tiny memorial bench near her old metro station for tired travelers to rest.
all.zh: 我弟弟在她以前常去的地鐵站附近設計了一張小紀念長椅，讓疲倦的旅人歇腳。

all.en: The bench bore a simple plaque saying: For everyone who has ever felt lost and been gently found.
all.zh: 長椅上有一塊簡單的銘牌寫著：獻給每個曾迷失又被溫柔尋回的人。

all.en: On rainy afternoons, strangers shared umbrellas on that bench and quietly began new friendships.
all.zh: 在下雨的午後，陌生人在那張長椅上共撐傘，悄悄開啟新的友誼。

all.en: Her daughter and I aged into trusted elders for the entire residential street and beyond.
all.zh: 她的女兒和我逐漸成為整條住宅街道乃至更遠地方都信賴的長者。

all.en: On peaceful evenings, we walked slowly past her old apartment and whispered a small thank-you upward.
all.zh: 在寧靜的傍晚，我們慢步走過她以前的公寓，向上輕聲說一句小小的謝謝。
